landing.title: Alliance HQ
landing.tagline: Alliance tools for Last War officers
landing.purposeBody: Alliance HQ at frontline.gay is a hosted community tool for Last War alliance officers. It is a convenience layer on top of ashed.online — your alliance data stays on Ashed; HQ helps your team run day-to-day operations with a modern web app and Discord integration.
landing.featureRoster: Sync and manage your alliance roster from Ashed
landing.featureTrains: Plan and track alliance trains with officer workflows
landing.featureVrDiscord: VR standings and Discord bot commands for your guild
landing.featureInvites: Team invites and role-based access for officers and members
landing.featureVideo: Upload battle recordings for OCR-assisted score entry

form.name: Name
form.passKeyHelp: Short machine identifier used in analytics (e.g. scene_0.33). Must be unique.
form.modeHelp: Scene detection extracts frames only when the image changes significantly — good for scoreboards. FPS extracts a fixed number of frames per second — simpler but may miss or duplicate frames.
form.sceneThresholdHelp: How different two frames must be for a new frame to be captured. Lower values (e.g. 0.1) = more frames, slower, catches subtle changes. Higher values (e.g. 0.5) = fewer frames, faster, may miss small score updates.
form.sampleFpsHelp: Frames captured per second (for FPS mode, or as a cap for scene mode). Higher = more thorough but more storage and processing cost.

embeddedLogin.optionalBody: Skip this step if you already sign into ashed.online with email and password.
embeddedLogin.problemIntro: Alliance HQ embeds ashed.online inside the app. If you sign into Ashed with Google, Google blocks that login inside embedded pages — you may see a 403 error when the iframe asks you to sign in.
embeddedLogin.workaroundIntro: You can add a password to your Ashed account once. After that, use email + password for embedded pages in Alliance HQ. Your Google login still works when you open ashed.online directly.
embeddedLogin.stepForgotPassword: On ashed.online (same tab from step 1), open the login screen and choose Forgot password? Enter the email on your Ashed account.
embeddedLogin.stepResetEmail: Open the reset link from your email and choose a new password.
embeddedLogin.stepChoosePassword: Confirm the password is saved — you will use it inside Alliance HQ, not your Google account.
embeddedLogin.stepFirstEmbedLogin: After you connect below, the first embedded Ashed page may ask you to log in. Use email + password — do not click Sign in with Google.
embeddedLogin.googleStillWorksNote: This does not remove Google Sign-In from ashed.online when you visit it in its own tab.
embeddedLogin.checklistHint: Not required — use Skip if this does not apply to you.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Alliance HQ at frontline.gay is a community tool for Last War alliance officers. This page explains what we store and how we protect it.
privacy.noSellTitle: We do not sell your data
privacy.noSellBody1: Alliance HQ exists to help your alliance use ashed.online more easily. We will never sell, rent, or trade your personal information or alliance data to third parties. We do not use your data for advertising, and we do not build marketing profiles from your activity.
privacy.noSellBody2: We only use the information needed to run the service you asked for — for example, keeping you signed in and calling Ashed on your behalf when you use Alliance HQ features.

whatWeStoreItems.session: A random session identifier in an httpOnly cookie in your browser (not your Ashed login token).
whatWeStoreItems.token: Your Ashed connection key (JWT), encrypted on our server — see below.
whatWeStoreItems.metadata: Basic connection metadata, such as when your token expires and your reminder preferences.
whatWeStoreItems.label: An optional display label (for example, your Ashed account name) so we can show who is connected.

privacy.whatWeStoreFooter: Alliance reports, member lists, and other alliance content stay on Ashed's systems. Alliance HQ does not copy that data into a separate database for resale or unrelated purposes.
privacy.encryptionTitle: How your Ashed connection key is protected
privacy.encryptionIntro: When you connect Alliance HQ to ashed.online, you paste a network request that contains a login token (JWT). That token is the key that lets Alliance HQ talk to Ashed as you. We treat it like a password.

encryptionSteps.encrypted: Encrypted before storage. As soon as we receive your token, we encrypt it with AES-256-GCM (industry-standard authenticated encryption) before writing anything to our database.
encryptionSteps.separateKey: Separate encryption key. The key used to encrypt your token lives only in our server environment — it is never stored in the database alongside your encrypted token, and it is never sent to your browser.
encryptionSteps.uniqueIv: Unique ciphertext each time. Each encryption uses a fresh random value (IV), so even identical tokens would not produce identical stored data.
encryptionSteps.serverSide: Server-side only. Your browser receives a short-lived session cookie. The Ashed JWT itself stays on our server and is decrypted only when Alliance HQ needs to call Ashed for you.
encryptionSteps.disconnect: You can disconnect. Settings → Disconnect removes your stored connection key from our server.

privacy.whereDataBody: Alliance HQ runs on managed cloud hosting (Vercel for the app, Neon Postgres for encrypted credentials and session data). Data is transmitted over HTTPS.
privacy.questionsBody: This policy may be updated as Alliance HQ grows. If you have questions about your data, open an issue on our <githubLink>GitHub repository</githubLink>.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: Alliance HQ at frontline.gay is a community tool for Last War alliance officers. By using this hosted service, you agree to these terms.
terms.acceptableUseBody1: Use Alliance HQ and <link>ashed.online</link> reasonably and lawfully. Alliance HQ is a convenience layer on top of Ashed — it does not grant rights beyond what you already have on Ashed itself.

acceptableUseItems.noAbuse: Do not abuse, overload, or disrupt Alliance HQ or Ashed (for example automated scraping beyond normal use, credential stuffing, or attempts to bypass security).
acceptableUseItems.noHarm: Do not harass other users, alliance officers, or operators of this service.
acceptableUseItems.noMisuse: Do not use Alliance HQ in ways that harm alliances, Ashed, or other players.

terms.enforcementBody: Operators of this hosted deployment reserve the right to revoke or block access — including sessions, linked devices, and Discord bot linkage — for violations without prior warning. Self-hosted copies of Alliance HQ are outside our control; these enforcement terms apply only to deployments we operate (such as frontline.gay).
terms.openSourceBody1: Alliance HQ source code is open under the MIT License. You may inspect, modify, and self-host the software under that license.
terms.openSourceBody2: See the <licenseLink>LICENSE</licenseLink> file in our <githubLink>GitHub repository</githubLink>. These Terms of Service apply to our hosted service, not to independent self-hosted instances you run yourself.
terms.disclaimerBody: Alliance HQ is a community wrapper around Ashed, provided as is without warranties. Using ashed.online directly remains subject to Ashed’s own terms and policies.
terms.changesBody: We may update these terms as Alliance HQ evolves. Continued use of the hosted service after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.
terms.questionsBody: If you have questions about these terms, open an issue on our <githubLink>GitHub repository</githubLink>.

memberOnboarding.title: Member onboarding
memberOnboarding.selfServiceDescription: When this is on, members who join through an invite or join code can link their commander right away — even when no roster row matched at link time. Alliance HQ verifies their player UID with Last War first and uses that verified in-game name. If HQ had to add a new roster row, officers can later confirm the link or merge it with an existing unlinked entry when the same person may already be on the roster under an older name.
memberOnboarding.selfServiceEnabledLabel: Allow invited members to link without waiting for approval
memberOnboarding.selfServiceActiveNotice: Self-service linking is on. Verified Last War names are used automatically. New roster rows are created when needed; officers review those links afterward to confirm or merge duplicates.

discordServerSetup.title: Discord bot — add another server
discordServerSetup.body: Your alliance can link multiple Discord servers to the same bot. An alliance owner, R4+ officer, or platform maintainer can add the bot to a Discord server they own or can manage, then register that server to your alliance tag so slash commands work there.

discordServerSetup.nextStepsBody:
In the new Discord server:
1. Open the install wizard from Alliance HQ (Settings → Discord or /discord/setup).
2. Run /link-commander in Discord after install finishes.
3. Run /set-vr-report-channel and /set-train-channel in the channels you want.

Discord requires Manage Server permission to add the bot. Slash commands may take a few minutes to appear after install.

trains.title: Alliance Train
trains.subtitle: Choo-choo, baby!
trains.emptyRosterBody: Import your alliance roster before planning train schedules or rolling for Conductor.

planWeekBanner.title: Plan this train week
planWeekBanner.body: This week is a draft from your default template. Pick a template, paint days, or roll today's conductor — your plan saves when you take action.

trains.previewLegend: Faded days are drafts; they save when an officer updates the schedule.

clearWeekSchedule.title: Clear this week’s schedule?
clearWeekSchedule.body: Removes the saved schedule for {weekStart} – {weekEnd} and returns it to draft preview.
clearWeekSchedule.hint: Conductor rolls and locks are kept. Pre-production only.

trains.paintPaletteHint: Select days on the calendar (tap, drag, or Shift+click a range), then choose a template to paint them.

trainIsReady.confirm: Lock **{name}** for **{date}** and announce in Discord?

trains.unlockConfirm: Return this day to draft? The empty train will be removed.
trains.nativeAshedBanner: This day uses live VS or donation scores from Ashed. Connect Ashed in Settings, or pick conductor/VIP manually from your roster.
trains.nativeModeBanner: This alliance runs trains in native mode. VS and donation rolls need a connected Ashed roster; use manual picks when scores are unavailable.

serverTimeClock.timezoneNote: Train days and lock timing follow game server time (UTC−2), not your account timezone.

pastPaintConfirm.title: Paint past train days?
pastPaintConfirm.body: You are about to apply {template} to {count} past day(s). Locked conductors are still protected server-side.
pastPaintConfirm.hint: Officers cannot paint past days without platform maintainer access.

help.title: Alliance Train help
help.serverTimeBody: Last War uses fixed server time (UTC−2, no daylight saving). Train days, VS weeks, and lock deadlines all follow this clock — not your local timezone.

quickStart.paint: Pick a week template or paint individual days on the schedule.
quickStart.roll: Spin the wheel or pick a conductor for today and upcoming days.
quickStart.lock: Lock in a conductor to spawn that day's empty train.

templateChangeConfirm.title: Change week template?
templateChangeConfirm.body: The rest of this week ({startDate} through {endDate}) will switch to the {template} template.
templateChangeConfirm.bodyWithLocks: Days through {cutoffDate} stay unchanged because the Conductor is locked on those days. {startDate} through {endDate} will switch to the {template} template.
templateChangeConfirm.noDatesBody: No unlocked days remain in this week to repaint.

pivotBanner.title: Pivot to economy week?
pivotBanner.body: After Day 1 VS, you can switch Tue–Sun to economy-style R3 lottery so members stop burning inventory on a lost week.

spinWeek.disqualifiedExhausted: Could not find a qualified conductor after several spins. Try again or pick manually.
spinWeek.confirmBody: Review {count} conductors drawn from the pool. Locking spawns empty trains for each day.

disabledReason.pastWeek: This train week is entirely in the past.
disabledReason.noEligibleDays: No remaining days in this week need a wheel spin.

walkthrough.stepServerTime: Train days follow game server time (UTC−2). The badge above always shows the current server date and time — use it when planning rolls and locks.
walkthrough.stepSchedule: This is your train scheduling hub — pick a week, paint day templates, and see who is conducting each day.
walkthrough.stepWeekStrip: Each cell shows the day template color, conductor mechanism, and whether the conductor is locked in.
walkthrough.stepTemplate: Change the whole week’s template from this dropdown — economy, lottery, VS week, and more.
walkthrough.stepViewToggle: Switch between week and month view. Month view is best for painting templates ahead.
walkthrough.stepConductorCard: Today’s conductor card shows the VIP, guardian, and lock status. Locking spawns the train for that day.
walkthrough.stepQuickActions: Roll or spin the wheel, pick the top scorer, or choose someone manually from quick actions.
walkthrough.stepSpinWeek: Spin for the Week rolls and locks every remaining wheel-eligible day in one pass.

swap.title: Swap conductors
swap.body: Move an unlocked conductor draft to another day or swap between two unlocked days — useful for make-up days. Assigned target days will be locked after you confirm.
swap.noCandidates: No other days in this week to swap with.
swap.previewMoveToOpen: {nameA} moves to {dayB}. {dayA} will have no conductor.

reseedPoolHint.title: Re-seed pool
reseedPoolHint.body: Lottery and sequence rolls draw from a pool of eligible members. Each roll marks someone as picked for the current generation. Re-seeding rebuilds that list from your roster and immediately begins a new generation -— useful after rank changes, or when everyone in the pool has had their turn.

poolRefreshedHint.title: Pool refreshed
poolRefreshedHint.conductorBody: Everyone in the {poolName} has had a turn as Conductor. The pool was rebuilt automatically for the next rotation.
poolRefreshedHint.vipBody: Everyone in the {poolName} has had a turn as VIP. The pool was rebuilt automatically for the next rotation.

trains.emptyPoolScheduleBlocked: Import alliance members before creating a train schedule.

spinSource.heading: Spin sources

spinSource.liveLeaderboardHint: Drawn from live scores, not the member pool.

poolDetails.emptyChosen: No one has been picked from this pool yet.
poolDetails.emptyUnseeded: Pool not built yet — spin or re-seed to create it.
poolDetails.eventScoreContext: VS Day {dayNumber}: {dayName} · scores from {scoreDate} (yesterday's VS day)

conductorHistory.title: Conductor history
conductorHistory.empty: Locked conductor days will appear here.

wheelBlocked.title: Can't spin the wheel
wheelBlocked.poolEmptyR3: No members on your roster are ranked R3, so the lottery pool is empty. Assign R3 ranks on the Members page, then try again.
wheelBlocked.poolEmptyR4Plus: No members on your roster are ranked R4 or R5, so the officer sequence pool is empty. Assign those ranks on the Members page, then try again.
wheelBlocked.poolEmptyGeneric: No eligible members were found for this pool. Check member ranks on the Members page, then try again.
wheelBlocked.poolExhausted: Everyone eligible for this pool has already been picked in the current generation. Re-seed the pool to start a fresh rotation.

wheelBlocked.noVsScores: There aren't enough VS scores to fill the wheel yet. Enter VS scores for this week, then try again.

wheelBlocked.noDonationScores: Donation scores aren't tracked in Alliance HQ yet. Pick the donor manually from your roster.
wheelBlocked.noCandidatesGeneric: There aren't enough eligible members to spin the wheel right now.
wheelBlocked.ashedRequired: This roll needs an Ashed connection to read live scores. Connect Ashed in Settings, or use a roster-based mechanism instead.
wheelBlocked.generic: The wheel can't spin right now. Fix the underlying issue and try again.

templateDetails.vs_push_week: Conductor: Tue and Fri = prior-day VS #1 (auto); Wed, Thu, and Sat = random draw from VS top 10; Sun–Mon = R4+ officer rotation. VIP: Conductor picks VIP on VS weekdays; event top-10 lottery Sun–Mon.
templateDetails.vs_push_weekdays: Conductor: Mon = top donor; Tue and Fri = prior-day VS #1; Wed, Thu, and Sat = random from VS top 10. VIP: Mon = 2nd donor; Tue–Sat = conductor free pick.
templateDetails.r4_event_vip: Conductor: R4+ officer rotation. VIP: Event top-10 lottery wheel (default event: Capitol War). Typical for weekend train days.
templateDetails.economy_week: Conductor: R3 wheel every day — full R3 pool, no VS score band. VIP: Today's conductor picks VIP directly (free pick). Use to pivot mid-week when pushing less VS.
templateDetails.price_is_right: Conductor: R3 wheel draw from members whose prior-day VS score is between 7,200,000 and the economy threshold plus fudge. VIP: Today's conductor picks VIP directly (free pick).
templateDetails.r3_recognition: Conductor: R3 wheel every day — full R3 pool (same draw as Economy week, without VS filtering). VIP: Today's conductor picks VIP directly (free pick).
templateDetails.r4_train_week: Conductor: R4+ officer rotation — walks the pool in order, one conductor per day. VIP: Today's conductor picks VIP directly (free pick).
templateDetails.donations_week: Conductor: Top alliance donor (auto). VIP: Second-place donor (auto; officers can override if leaderboard data is wrong).
templateDetails.custom: Conductor / VIP: No preset rules — set each day's mechanisms on the calendar or pick manually when you roll.

video.categoryLabel: What kind of scoreboard is this?

video.fileSizeTipsIntro: If the file is too large, try:

video.fileSizeTipDownscale: As a last resort, downscale the video resolution.

memberLink.walkthroughDone: Great. Submit your name and UID again when ready.
memberLink.walkthroughIntro: Let's get your in-game name right.
memberLink.walkthroughTapDone: Tap Done when you've finished this step.
memberLink.nameMismatchIntro: Your name didn't match what Last War has on file. Follow these steps — the game does not let you copy your own name.
steps: Open Last War and go to your Player Profile (tap your avatar, then … in the top right).
Copy your UID from that screen — you can copy your own player ID.
Last War does not let you copy your own name. Ask an alliance mate to open your profile and tap Copy next to your name, then message you.
Type your name exactly as they sent it (spelling and capitalization matter), or paste if they copied it for you.
Return here and submit that name with your UID.

If you're the maintainer, remove `DISCORD_ALLIANCE_ID` from the deployment env — multi-tenant bots resolve alliance by server registration, not a global env var.
errors.unknownUser: Could not identify your Discord account.
errors.serverError: Something went wrong on our side. Try again in a moment.
errors.guildNotRegistered: This Discord server is not linked to an alliance yet. An alliance owner or R4+ officer should run `/link-alliance` with your tag (for example LFgo).
errors.tagNotFoundNoHq: No alliance found with tag **{tag}**. Check the spelling on ashed.online. If you're the owner, run `/link` in Discord first, then run `/help` here.
errors.tagNotFoundWithHq: No alliance found with tag **{tag}**. Check the spelling on ashed.online, or run `/link-ashed tag:{tag}` if your alliance is not in Alliance HQ yet.
errors.tagRequired: Add your alliance tag. Example: `/link-alliance tag:LFgo` or `/link-ashed tag:LFgo`.
errors.tagNotEligible: Alliance tag **{tag}** is not currently eligible for bot setup on this deployment.
errors.linkAllianceNeedCommander: Link your commander with `/link-commander`, then run `/link-alliance tag:{tag}`. **Optional:** `/link-ashed tag:{tag}` enables live Ashed roster sync.
errors.linkHqFirst: Link your Alliance HQ account first — run `/link` in Discord, then run `/help` for setup steps.
errors.tagAmbiguous: We found multiple alliances with tag **{tag}**. Reply with `/link-alliance tag:{tag} name:Your Alliance Name` to pick the right one.
errors.notOwner: Only your **R5 (alliance owner)**, an R4+ officer with a linked commander, the person who linked Ashed credentials for this tag, or a platform maintainer can register this server. Run `/help` or see Getting started for R5 on Alliance HQ.
errors.notOfficer: Only alliance officers (R4+) or the owner can run VR reports. Link a commander with `/link-commander` if you haven't yet.
errors.insufficientForTeams: Need at least **{needed}** VR reporters for **{teams}** takedown teams (5 players each). Only **{have}** on the board right now.
errors.teamsRequired: Add `teams:N` (1–5). Example: `/takedown-teams teams:2` for two rally teams.
errors.teamsTooMany: At most **{max}** takedown teams per request. Example: `/vr-report teams:3`.

gettingStarted.title: Getting started — R5
gettingStarted.description: R5 setup for Alliance HQ: web-first or Discord-first, with Ashed sync when you have it.

hub.title: Discord bot setup guide
hub.subtitle: Connect your alliance Discord server to Alliance HQ so members can report base VR, officers can run standings, and owners can turn on nightly posts.
hub.overviewBody: Alliance HQ adds a bot to your Discord server. It does not replace your alliance leadership — it gives you slash commands for in-game tasks (link your commander, report VR, train conductor tools) while keeping sensitive steps on a secure web page when needed. You never paste passwords or connection keys into Discord.
hub.prerequisitesBody:
• You need permission to use slash commands in the server.
• R5 needs their alliance tag (letters only, e.g. LFgo).
• Everyone linking a character needs their player UID from the Last War profile screen.
hub.r5Callout: <link>Getting started for R5</link> — new alliance? Start with web-first or Discord-first setup before this checklist.
hub.rolePrompt: What's your role in your alliance?
hub.pickRole: Choose a path — you can switch anytime.

r5.title: R5 — alliance owner
r5.description: Install wizard, link your commander, set VR and train channels, then tell members to link.

r4.title: R4 — alliance officer
r4.description: Add the bot to another server, link your commander, register the server, then run officer commands and help members.

member.title: R3–R1 — alliance member
member.description: Link your commander and use member commands like /vr and /who-is-conductor.

link-only.title: I just want to link my commander
link-only.description: The server is already set up. You only need /link-commander with your player UID.

flow.title: Your setup path
flow.recoveryBody: Use these slash-command steps only if you added the bot before the install wizard or need to finish from Discord alone.
flow.tapStep: Tap a step for detailed instructions.

installBot.title: Add the bot to your Discord server
installBot.summary: One web wizard: HQ account, Ashed sync, and bot install — your Discord server is linked when install finishes.
installBot.instructions:
**New alliance? R5 start here:** Getting started for R5 at /guides/getting-started.

Use the **install wizard** at /discord/setup — sign in, link Discord, enter your alliance tag, connect Ashed, then add the bot. When Discord sends you back, your server is registered automatically.

After that, run **`/link-commander`** in Discord.

linkHq.title: Link your Discord to Alliance HQ
linkHq.summary: Recovery path — sign your Discord account into Alliance HQ.
linkHq.instructions: **Recovery path.** If you added the bot before using the install wizard, run **`/link`** in Discord to sign in, then use the install wizard at /discord/setup or **`/link-alliance`**.

linkSelf.title: Link your commander
linkSelf.summary: Match your in-game character with your player UID — confirm the commander name when prompted.
linkSelf.instructions:
Your Discord server must be registered first (the install wizard does this automatically).

In Discord:
1. Run /link-commander.
2. Open the secure link and enter your player ID from your Last War profile.
3. Confirm the commander name when prompted.

You can link up to 5 commanders per Discord account. Use /unlink to remove one.
linkSelf.tip: Copy your player UID from your Last War profile menu. Double-check the digits before you tap Yes.

registerGuild.title: Register this Discord server
registerGuild.summary: Recovery path — manually bind this Discord server to your alliance.
registerGuild.instructions: **Recovery path.** The install wizard at /discord/setup registers your server automatically. Manual: **`/link-alliance tag:YourTag`** when you are proven as R5, officer, or Ashed credential registrant.

optionalAshed.title: Optional: roster sync tools
optionalAshed.summary: Recovery path — connect Ashed credentials for roster sync.
optionalAshed.instructions: **Recovery path.** Prefer the install wizard at /discord/setup or Connect Ashed at /connect. From Discord only: **`/link-ashed tag:YourTag`** → on-page walkthrough.

vrChannel.title: Nightly VR report channel
vrChannel.summary: Choose where the bot posts the top-25 VR standings each night.
vrChannel.instructions:
1. Open the Discord channel where members should see nightly VR standings.
2. Run /set-vr-report-channel in that channel.
3. The bot will post automatically on the configured schedule.

trainChannel.title: Train announcement channel
trainChannel.summary: Choose where train conductor lock-in posts appear, and turn on announcements in Alliance HQ.
trainChannel.instructions:
1. Run /set-train-channel in the channel where members should see train posts.
2. In Alliance HQ (signed in), open Settings → Alliance settings for your tag.
3. Enable Post train announcements to Discord.

Both the channel and the HQ toggle must be on before public train posts appear.

tellMembers.title: Tell members to link
tellMembers.summary: Share the member path so roster members can link commanders and report VR.
tellMembers.instructions:
Post a short note in your alliance channel:
• Run /link-commander and complete the secure link (player ID from the in-game profile).
• Confirm the commander name when prompted.
• Use /vr or /immunity when your base VR changes.

serverPrereq.title: Confirm the server is registered
serverPrereq.summary: Officer commands only work after the alliance owner registers this Discord server.
serverPrereq.instructions:
Ask your R5 owner or an R4+ officer to complete setup: add the bot (Alliance HQ alliance settings), /link, optional /link-ashed tag:YourTag, then /link-alliance tag:YourTag, then link their commander.

If /link-commander says the server is not set up, registration has not finished yet.

vrReports.title: VR standings and takedown teams
vrReports.summary: Officers can pull standings and balanced rally teams from Discord.
vrReports.instructions:
1. Link your commander first — you must be R4+ in-game on the linked character (or alliance owner).
2. Run /vr-report for the top-25 standings (ephemeral reply).
3. Run /vr-report teams:5 or /takedown-teams teams:5 for snake-balanced rally teams.

trainOps.title: Train conductor workflow
trainOps.summary: Pick, confirm, and lock today's train conductor from Discord.
trainOps.instructions:
1. /set-conductor name:ExactName saves a draft conductor (confirm with the buttons).
2. /who-is-conductor shows today's draft or locked conductor.
3. /train-is-ready locks the conductor and posts to the train channel when announcements are enabled.

See the Discord train operator guide in Alliance HQ for the full daily lifecycle.

helpMembers.title: Help members who are stuck
helpMembers.summary: Point members to the link-only guide path when they cannot get their UID or confirm identity.
helpMembers.instructions:
Common fixes:
• Wrong commander on confirm — tap No and run /link-commander again with the correct player ID on the secure page.
• Roster miss — refresh the roster on Alliance HQ or approve their link as an officer.
• Still stuck — share the link-only role path from this guide, or help them use Ask an officer.

reportVr.title: Report base VR
reportVr.summary: Tell the bot when your base viral resistance changes.
reportVr.instructions:
1. Link your commander first.
2. Run /vr or /immunity when your base VR changes.
3. If you have multiple linked commanders, the bot will ask which one.

checkConductor.title: Who is conducting today?
checkConductor.summary: Check today's train conductor without officer permissions.
checkConductor.instructions: Run /who-is-conductor in the alliance server. The reply is private to you and shows the conductor, VIP if set, and whether the day is draft or locked.

language.title: Switch language
language.summary: Use the bot in English or Portuguese.
language.instructions: Run /language and pick English or Português. Your choice is saved for future commands.

afterLink.title: After you're linked
afterLink.summary: What you can do once your commander is linked.
afterLink.instructions:
• /vr or /immunity — report base VR when it changes.
• /who-is-conductor — see today's train conductor.
• /help — context-aware tips for your situation.
• /language — switch to Português.
• /unlink — remove a linked commander if you linked the wrong character.

copyNameUid.title: Getting your player UID
copyNameUid.body:
Open Last War → tap your avatar → tap … (top right) on your profile.

• **Player ID:** tap Copy on your player ID — you can copy your own ID.

Run /link-commander on Discord, open the secure link, enter that ID, then confirm the commander name when prompted.

nameMismatch.title: Wrong commander on confirm
nameMismatch.body: You tapped No on the identity prompt, or the name didn't look right. Run /link-commander again, enter the correct player ID on the secure page, and confirm only when the commander name matches you.

rosterMiss.title: Name not on the roster yet
rosterMiss.body: Last War verified your UID, but the alliance roster does not list that name yet (often after a rename). Ask an R4+ officer or the owner to refresh the roster on Alliance HQ or approve your link.

guildNotRegistered.title: Bot says the server isn't set up
guildNotRegistered.body: An R5 owner or R4+ officer (or platform maintainer) must run /link-alliance tag:YourTag first. Until then, /link-commander will not attach to your alliance.

commandsMissing.title: Slash commands not showing up
commandsMissing.body:
After adding the bot to a new server, Discord may take a few minutes to register global slash commands.

Try:
• Wait 5–15 minutes and type /link in the server again.
• Confirm the bot appears online in the member list.
• If commands never appear, ask a maintainer to verify command registration for the bot application.

memberTaken.title: Commander already linked
memberTaken.body: That in-game character is linked to a different Discord account. The bot cannot transfer links — contact your alliance leadership if this was a mistake.

notOfficer.title: Officer command denied
notOfficer.body: /vr-report, /set-conductor, and /train-is-ready require R4+ on the linked commander (or alliance owner). Link the character that holds officer rank in-game.

wrongServer.title: Wrong state server
wrongServer.body: Your UID belongs to a different state server than this alliance. Confirm you joined the correct alliance with your R5.

copyNameUid.alt: Last War profile screen showing the Copy button on your player UID
copyNameUid.caption: Copy your player UID from your profile, then confirm the commander name in Discord.

uploadReminders.title: Upload reminders
uploadReminders.subtitle: Alliance officers see reminders in the inbox when uploads are due. Upload processors see a separate queue when videos are ready to submit.
uploadReminders.personalBody: Opt in to personal reminders for score targets you care about.
uploadReminders.allianceBody: Officers with schedule access can define when the alliance is reminded to upload each event.
